mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold crossing fails to predict downward price momentum in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of household savings and rising inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power, forcing a sharp pullback in discretionary spending that reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS decline + CPIAUCSL rise) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns, or positive returns dominate during simultaneous savings and inflation shocks.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, a contraction in industrial production directly translates to lower corporate revenue growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because firms cannot offset higher financing costs with volume growth.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-rate periods do not predict negative 20-day forward returns, or the predictive relationship reverses sign in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples expand alongside fundamental growth.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently fail to correlate with 60-day forward equity returns, or positive GDP growth periods yield negative SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly payroll employment exceeds 200,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms robust labor demand that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The threshold crossing fails to generate positive forward returns in >55% of instances, or the signal produces significant false positives during late-cycle overheating phases.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and decline in housing starts signals a credit-driven demand shock that restricts mortgage availability and wealth effects, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as financial conditions tighten.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS increase and HOUST decline do not predict negative 20-day forward returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a sustained decline in the unemployment rate combined with rising industrial production indicates a healthy labor-supply alignment that sustains corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE decline and INDPRO rise fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns, or the signal reverses to negative during supply-constrained recovery phases.
